tests.intent: Two session-bound SQL table macros expose every opaque masked or billboard candidate, all clipping facts, and deterministic winning overlay pixels.
Transparent two-sided middle textures preserve opaque posts, discard holes, use sidedef offsets, and remain depth-bearing overlay candidates.
Billboards use bound camera pose, configured projection, patch offsets, world height, pixel centers, and deterministic integer screen bounds.
Viewer-relative object bearing selects all eight directional rotations at half-sector boundaries with rotation-zero fallback only when declared.
Dual-name sprite lumps choose the independently recorded rotation and horizontal mirror without changing transparent sampling semantics.
Absent patch texels never overwrite the world while palette index zero remains an opaque, lightable source color rather than transparency.
Projected samples outside columns zero through 319 or rows zero through 199 are rejected without shrinking the canonical resolution.
Sprite samples are clipped to the active per-column sector window inherited from the complete portal interval timeline.
A nearer solid wall or closed portal hides a sprite while a nearer sprite remains visible and equal wall depth follows the fixed epsilon rule.
At each covered pixel the nearest eligible sprite candidate occludes all farther sprites without object insertion-order dependence.
Equal depth resolves by source class, stable source id, asset row, and asset column so identical poses remain byte-deterministic.
Every renderable state reachable from E1M1 decorations, pickups, monsters, barrels, projectiles, and effects resolves to a seeded sprite rotation asset.
Spawn and two diagnostic poses expose exact independently checked sprite identities, rotations, clipped counts, spot pixels, and canonical overlay hashes.
Joint translation of camera and world objects preserves projected candidates, rotations, clipping decisions, and winning pixel documents.
Production performs projection, transparent sampling, clipping, and depth selection in bound set-based SQL with no procedural object or pixel loops.
Production cannot inspect evaluator artifacts, embed diagnostic poses or hashes, use dynamic SQL, or forge evaluator outcomes.
Every isolated masked-wall, billboard, rotation, transparency, clipping, ordering, closure, and anti-cheat mutation has a named semantic witness.
